88.reason: [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read at end of file; also, initial value of w is not set to empty string, and logic fails to terminate word on non-alphabetic chars that are not whitespace]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read at end of file; also, initial value of w is not set to empty string, and logic fails to terminate word on non-alphabetic chars that are not whitespace]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read at end-of-file; function may not null-terminate the word or return properly, leading to undefined behavior. Also, using char for fgetc() return value causes EOF comparison issues because char may be unsigned.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read at end of file; also, the logic for detecting word boundaries is flawed and may not terminate words correctly in all cases, leading to undefined behavior or infinite loops. Additionally, the function does not return EOF properly when no word is read at EOF.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read; if file ends without delimiter, last word is not null-terminated nor returned]
